title: Help
subtitle: Understand NoteSentry's architecture, how it works, and how to use it. Prefer a guided walkthrough? Start the feature tour.

arch.title: System architecture
arch.desc: Four hospital information pillars integrated via MCP. All inference runs on local Ollama; every tool call runs only after your approval and is written to the audit log.
arch.localNote: Data and inference stay on this device — no upload, no sharing, no telemetry.

flow.title: Three-layer defense (data flow)
flow.step1: MCP grounding: deterministic tools (lookups, rules, official standards) provide the facts, so the model doesn't fabricate.
flow.step2: LLM reasoning: the local model composes the answer only from what the tools returned.
flow.step3: Human review: you approve each tool call before it runs; every call and result is written to the audit log.

hitl.title: Why does every tool call need human approval?
hitl.p1: Lower risk: the model can misunderstand or fabricate; review catches inappropriate queries before they run.
hitl.p2: Accountability: every action is human-approved and traceable in the audit log.
hitl.p3: Compliance: MIMIC-III is governed by the PhysioNet DUA; data access must be controlled and auditable.
hitl.p4: Educational: a clear demonstration of human-in-the-loop design and AI governance.

shortcuts.desc: Switch views (Dashboard / Chat / Apps / Tools / Audit / Settings)
Stop generating / close the palette or dialog
Approval dialog: Enter approves, Esc rejects

faq.a: To keep data on-device, the Ollama URL is restricted to localhost / 127.0.0.1; non-local addresses are blocked. All inference happens on your machine.
It means the model actually called a tool but the database had no matching records. That's intentional grounding — it states the truth rather than fabricating.

faq.q: How do I add a custom MCP server?
faq.a: Settings → MCP servers tab → Add server, then fill in the standard command / args / env (or paste a standard mcpServers JSON to import). Saving connects it and runs a conformance check.

faq.q: How do I export a case report?
faq.a: On the chat header, click Case report and choose Markdown or HTML in the save dialog; the HTML is self-contained, print-friendly, and syntax-highlighted.

faq.q: Can I tune the AI's behavior?
faq.a: Settings → AI behavior tab lets you adjust the sampling temperature and max tool turns, and preview the actual system prompt.

faq.q: Does my data leave the device?
faq.a: No. Inference runs on local Ollama; conversations, logs, and settings are stored only on your machine — no upload, no telemetry.
